metadata_schema.boundary_policy: Production/public/rights caveats belong in Constraints / Risks instead of being repeated as the opening sentence of every doc.

current_focus.title: ClipPipeGen Review Console / Minimal Review Console v0
current_focus.next_action: Open docs/content_planning/operator_cockpit.html, use Review mode for the planning-label source item, then use Backlog mode for URL-waiting ideas or System mode for closed gate readback before any CPD rerun.

open_surfaces.label: Dashboard
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Start here for current focus, feature progress, active artifacts, and doc-health findings.

open_surfaces.label: CPD Operator Cockpit
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use first for current CPD review; it shows the CPD-12 Review Console with true Review/Backlog/System modes and planning-label provenance.

open_surfaces.label: Artifacts
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use after the dashboard when an artifact needs its registry entry or open command.

open_surfaces.label: Thank V2 Open Command Repair Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use after ED-10bc when the ED-10ba v2 MP4 exists but the default opener did not appear visibly; includes -SelectVideo and path-only diagnostics.

open_surfaces.label: Thank ED-10ba V2 Access Recovery Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use after ED-10bb when a later supervisor asks to open the verified Thank-host v2 specimen.

open_surfaces.label: Representative Micro-Scene V2 Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ba v2 specimen: why the source window changed to 38.50-50.40s, how the purpose label is diagnostic, and which approval/render gates remain closed.

open_surfaces.label: Open Representative Micro-Scene V2
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use later only when a supervisor asks to open the ED-10ba internal review v2 MP4; run -SelectVideo or -PrintPath if the default player does not appear visibly.

open_surfaces.label: Review-Frame Clarification Surface
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ax surface: what the ED-10au specimen asks a later reviewer to judge, what it is not asking, and which next axis should be used without a default rerender.

open_surfaces.label: Grill-me Adoption and Review-Frame Plan
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10aw plan: Grill-me is a bounded local adversarial helper, nested prompts are forbidden, and review-frame clarification is the first default route.

open_surfaces.label: Micro-Scene Observation Frame Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10av readback: the ED-10au specimen opened and reads as a real scene, but expectation/review-frame clarity and subtitle/player-UI overlap remain classified warnings.

open_surfaces.label: Representative Micro-Scene Specimen Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10au source specimen consumed by ED-10av: actual Japanese transcript content, verified same-machine MP4 access, and closed production/public gates.

open_surfaces.label: Open Representative Micro-Scene Specimen
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use later only when a supervisor asks to open the ED-10au internal review specimen MP4.

open_surfaces.label: Internal Review Observation Readback
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10at user observation readback: openability and cue visibility pass diagnostically, chopped/memo-like continuity is not production review, and no approval is inferred.

open_surfaces.label: Internal Review Video Candidate Access Sheet
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to find current-host full paths and the launcher for the ED-10ar internal review video candidate.

open_surfaces.label: Open Internal Review Video Candidate
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use later only when a supervisor asks to open the internal review candidate MP4.

open_surfaces.label: Internal Review Video Candidate Package
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ar internal review video candidate package without treating it as production or public-use approval.

open_surfaces.label: Production Limitation Lift Stage 5 User Decision Ready
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10aq stage-5 user-decision-ready source packet without asking for approval now.

open_surfaces.label: Production Limitation-Lift Stage 3 Owner-Review Prep
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ao owner-review preparation packet that converts ED-10an decision groups into future freeform decision topics without requesting approval now.

open_surfaces.label: Production Limitation-Lift Stage 2 Decision Packet
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10an decision-preparation packet that groups ED-10am gates into subtitle design/visual acceptance, production render readiness, and rights/publishing/public-use clearance without approving those decisions.

open_surfaces.label: Production Limitation-Lift Stage 1
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10am gate matrix that turns ED-10al diagnostic rehearsal evidence into production/public decision preparation without approving those decisions.

open_surfaces.label: Final Render Path Stage 3 Rehearsal
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10al diagnostic rehearsal, generated ignored ASS/MP4/manifest outputs, output metadata, style survival readback, and closed production/public gates.

open_surfaces.label: Final Render Path Stage 2 Replayability
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ak operation packet, replay inputs, ignored outputs, command family, fresh-clone absence behavior, and closed production/public gates.

open_surfaces.label: Final Render Path Stage 1
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10aj selected FFmpeg/libass path, stage-1 checklist, and closed production/public gates.

open_surfaces.label: Final Render Path Readiness
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ai readiness matrix before any later final render-path stage or limitation-lift route.

open_surfaces.label: Production Limitation-Lift Entry
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ah gate matrix that separates diagnostic render-path proof from production, rights, publishing, and public-use decisions.

open_surfaces.label: Render Readiness Separation
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect what ED-10ag proves, what remains unaccepted, and which later explicit milestone may trigger a render.

open_surfaces.label: Render Path Selector Probe
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10af L2 selector probe rows, local ignored ASS/MP4/manifest paths, and boundary readback from the ED-10ae contract.

open_surfaces.label: Lineage Observation Surface
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the restored dry-read predecessor, active ED-10af L2 probe, and local ignored ASS/MP4/manifest/contact-sheet readback.

open_surfaces.label: Render Path Selector Contract
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect which selector, family, palette, color, motion, and line-break fields a later render adapter would receive; this is L0 static readback, not an actual render pass.

open_surfaces.label: Style Family Palette Proof
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ad family and palette axes while body subtitle text color remains stable and palette changes stay on badge, accent, and backplate surfaces.

open_surfaces.label: Visual Selector Proof
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use to inspect the ED-10ab semantic presets as badge, accent, backplate, size, motion, and line-break token differences while body subtitle text color stays stable.

open_surfaces.label: Preset Selector
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use before visual selector work to inspect deterministic speaker, emotion, intensity, utterance role, and readability token mappings.

open_surfaces.label: Style Intent Registry
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use before future subtitle style work so speaker, emotion, intensity, utterance role, and readability tags map to presets instead of repeated tiny numeric review loops.

open_surfaces.label: Tiny Render-Path Probe
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use as the ED-10z local readback that passes Candidate 2 through the current diagnostic render path while preserving Candidate 0 as fallback and Candidate 1/3 as held references.

open_surfaces.label: Multi-font Focused Review Reference
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use as the accepted ED-10o same-line comparison reference; it does not mean final baseline or production acceptance.

open_surfaces.label: Known Font Pack
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use as ED-10l/ED-10n readback evidence that the requested per-user fonts resolve in the regenerated comparison.

open_surfaces.label: BIZ Proof Reference
open_surfaces.command: see artifact registry for archived previous proof paths
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use only as the reviewed ED-10k reference that explains why the system-safe BIZ route stopped; open-current-proof now points to ED-10r.

open_surfaces.label: Font Audit
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use only to audit the consumed ED-10j contact sheet and candidate mapping.

open_surfaces.label: Gothic Balance
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use on the machine retaining ignored ED-10i comparison artifacts to judge gothic glyph body versus outline balance.

open_surfaces.label: Font Candidates
open_surfaces.when_to_use: Use when ED-10h font universe or local/system font availability is the next question.

active_artifacts.title: EWS-01 Episode Workspace Spine / Thin Gate Contract v0
active_artifacts.purpose: Convert the CPD-12 current Review item into a local episode workspace plan, with a compact contract that separates allowed local actions from deferred local actions and true external gates.
active_artifacts.storage_class: Tracked local planning artifact; portable JSON contract and workspace plan. No source fetch, media, transcript, render, thumbnail proof, OAuth/API, rights approval, publication, or tracked episodes/ material is created.
active_artifacts.review_status: Ready as the downstream local workspace spine. The plan carries episode_id=ep_seed_cpd01_bancho_marine_misunderstanding, the CPD-12 planning label, label_provenance=planning_label_unverified, source_url_state=present, identity_state=unverified, and fetch_authorized=false.
active_artifacts.next_action: Inspect or regenerate the plan, then use init-episode-workspace only with a tempdir or explicit ignored target if a local skeleton is needed before any source/media lane opens.

active_artifacts.title: ClipPipeGen Review Console / Minimal Review Console v0
active_artifacts.purpose: Convert the CPD operator cockpit from a weak view shell into a compact reusable review console with explicit fixed shell regions, dynamic data slots, true Review / Backlog / System modes, and planning-label provenance.
active_artifacts.storage_class: Tracked local planning artifact; portable CPD-01 through CPD-05 consolidation JSON/HTML. Supersedes the CPD-11 view-shell surface in the same output path.
active_artifacts.review_status: Ready as the normal human entry point for CPD planning review. The first viewport shows CPD-12 and the artifact id, a fixed shell label, status rail, true mode controls, and a Current Review data slot. The current item is labeled as a planning label with planning_label, source_url_present, identity_unverified, and not_fetched provenance badges. Backlog and System details stay behind their modes, and the Candidate Ledger remains readable and collapsed.
active_artifacts.next_action: Open the cockpit first, use Review mode for OK / NG / HOLD on the one planning-label source item, use Backlog mode for URL-waiting or hold records, and use System mode only for closed-gate/internal readback.

active_artifacts.title: ClipPipeGen Review Workbench / Operator View Shell v0
active_artifacts.purpose: Convert the CPD operator cockpit from a case-specific briefing page into a reusable view shell with Review, Backlog, and System modes driven by a content model.
active_artifacts.storage_class: Tracked local planning artifact; portable CPD-01 through CPD-05 consolidation JSON/HTML. Supersedes the CPD-10 one-page briefing/ledger surface in the same output path.
active_artifacts.review_status: Ready as the normal human entry point for CPD planning review. The first viewport is a reusable Review Workbench with state chips, a current source review item, OK / NG / HOLD labels, and a compact locked-gate line. Backlog and System details are separated from the default review mode, and the responsive Candidate Ledger remains available collapsed below.
active_artifacts.next_action: Open the cockpit first, use Review mode for the one source-ready item, use Backlog mode for URL-waiting or hold records, and use System mode only for closed-gate/internal readback.

active_artifacts.title: ClipPipeGen Operator Cockpit / Candidate Ledger Readability v0
active_artifacts.purpose: Preserve the accepted CPD-09 Briefing Board while making the lower Candidate Ledger readable for Japanese text-heavy candidate states.
active_artifacts.storage_class: Tracked local planning artifact; portable CPD-01 through CPD-05 consolidation JSON/HTML. Supersedes the CPD-09 ledger table in the same output path.
active_artifacts.review_status: Ready as the normal human entry point for CPD planning review after the ledger readability repair. The visible Candidate Ledger is stacked/responsive, keeps Japanese titles as full phrase lines, and de-emphasizes machine IDs in a code strip. Source-missing ideas remain not video-backed.
active_artifacts.next_action: Open the cockpit first, confirm the Candidate Ledger titles read normally, then inspect the single source-backed item through the Primary Review Script or fill source URLs for unresolved ideas before rerunning CPD-03 through CPD-10.
